When Saturn holds sway over a year, countries will be torn
by many battles of unruly bands of robbers, and deprived of their
cattle and wealth, with vast multiludes of people crying piteously
as a result of the murder of their near and dear ones, and of hunger
and fell diseases. The sky will be denuded of clouds driven by the
wind, all the trees with their branches broken down will cover the
earth; the sky will not be illuminated by the rays of the lumina-
ries, being covered with a dense mass of dust. Tanks and lakes
will dry up; and rivers will be very tiny. In some parts, crops will
perish for want of water, in others they grow being watered by
artificial means; and (or as) the Rain-god will yield but little rain.
